name: Myrddin Extended
description: Myrddin, often anglicized as Merlin, is a legendary figure prominent in Welsh mythology and Arthurian legend. He is traditionally regarded as a prophet, enchanter, and advisor. Myrddin's origins are shrouded in mystery, with various accounts attributing his birth to different circumstances, often involving supernatural elements. He is said to have been born of a mortal woman, Addan, and an incubus or some other supernatural entity. Unlike the purely Arthurian Merlin of later romances, the Welsh Myrddin is a more complex and fragmented figure, drawing on earlier traditions of wild men and prophets. He is associated with the forests of Wales, possessing a deep connection to nature and possessing immense magical power. He appears in early Welsh literature, most notably in the *Historia Brittonum* and the *Mabinogion*, as well as in later works such as Geoffrey of Monmouth's *Historia Regum Britanniae*, which popularized the figure of Merlin in Europe. Myrddin is known for his prophecies, his ability to shapeshift, his mastery of magic, and his role as a counselor to kings. He is often depicted as a wise and enigmatic figure, capable of both great good and terrible destruction. He retreats into the forest after witnessing the horrors of war, becoming a wild man, and gaining prophetic abilities. His story represents the power of nature, the dangers of unchecked ambition, and the enduring allure of the mystical. Myrddin's legacy continues to inspire and captivate audiences, solidifying his place as one of the most iconic figures in Welsh and Arthurian lore. The extended portion of his name here reflects the scope of his influence beyond just the character we see in Arthurian romance, encompassing the wider body of Welsh tradition.
subtitle: The Wild Prophet and Enchanter of Wales

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Myrddin
keyMyths.description: Myrddin's birth is attributed to a virgin princess and a supernatural entity, granting him extraordinary powers. This mysterious origin sets him apart as a figure of immense magical potential and prophetic ability. His mother was seduced by an incubus.

keyMyths.title: Myrddin's Madness
keyMyths.description: After witnessing the horrors of battle, Myrddin is driven mad and flees into the forest. There, he gains prophetic powers and a deep connection with nature, becoming a wild man and seer. This madness is a source of his wisdom.

keyMyths.title: Myrddin's Prophecies
keyMyths.description: Myrddin is renowned for his ability to foresee future events, often speaking in riddles and cryptic verses. His prophecies are highly valued by kings and those seeking guidance, but their interpretations are often complex. He foretold the coming of Arthur.

sources.author: Nennius
sources.description: An early Welsh historical text that contains one of the earliest accounts of Myrddin.
sources.text: An early Welsh historical text that contains one of the earliest accounts of Myrddin.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
sources.description: A highly influential, though largely fictional, account of British history that popularized the figure of Merlin.
sources.text: A highly influential, though largely fictional, account of British history that popularized the figure of Merlin.

sources.description: A 12th-century Latin poem that focuses on Myrddin's life in the forest.
